metadata.title: Tier 3 plugin-versioning sub-rubric + adapter category enum hygiene
metadata.description: Semver bump-rule rubric and adapter.schema.json category-enum hygiene rules for /gaia-review-code. Activates only when project-config.yaml declares project_kind: claude-code-plugin. Layer 2 (sub-rubric) of the four-layer rubric composition (base + sub + regime + project). The composite pre-deploy gate maps any non-APPROVE composite verdict — which includes the upstream UNVERIFIED → BLOCKED collapse — to deploy BLOCKED. NOTE on skill collision coordination: this sub-rubric and `plugin-code.json` both target skill=`code`. Per RFC 7396 array-replace semantics in rubric-merger.sh, the lexicographically-last sub-rubric's `severity_rules` array replaces all earlier ones. To avoid losing the plugin-code rules under skill=code, this rubric (which sorts after `plugin-code` in LC_ALL=C alpha order) prepends the three plugin-code rules verbatim before the seven plugin-versioning rules. The three carried-over rules are kept byte-identical to plugin-code.json — when plugin-code.json changes, this file MUST be updated to match (a tech-debt finding has been filed for an automated coordination check).

severity_rules.pattern: Plugin file with executable code reports line coverage below 80% (after applying any plugin-code.coverage_allowlist overrides from project-config.yaml).
severity_rules.description: Plugin projects default to 80% line coverage. Files below the threshold without an explicit allowlist override block merge. Use the plugin-code.coverage_allowlist key to grant per-path exemptions with a recorded rationale.
severity_rules.remediation: Either raise coverage on the offending paths to 80%, or — if the path is intentionally exempt — add an entry to plugin-code.coverage_allowlist with a threshold_percent and rationale.

severity_rules.pattern: Any SKILL.md file in the plugin tree measures more than 1500 tokens (soft ceiling). Approximate tokenizer: 4 chars per token.
severity_rules.description: A SKILL.md exceeding 1500 tokens trips a WARNING signal. The skill is approaching the 2000-token hard ceiling; trim verbose narrative, extract reasoning into the skill's playbook, or split into focused sub-skills before further additions.
severity_rules.remediation: Move long-form rationale into the playbook, prune redundant bullet lists, or split the skill along its TDD / planning / lifecycle seams. Re-measure with the same 4-chars-per-token approximation.

severity_rules.pattern: Any SKILL.md file in the plugin tree measures 2000 tokens or more (hard ceiling). Approximate tokenizer: 4 chars per token.
severity_rules.description: A SKILL.md at or above 2000 tokens MUST NOT merge. The skill exceeds the per-skill token budget that keeps Claude Code's planning context tractable. Split or trim the skill before re-running review.
severity_rules.remediation: Split the skill into smaller, focused skills (one workflow per skill) or extract long narrative into the playbook. Re-measure and confirm the total drops below 2000 tokens.

severity_rules.pattern: A SKILL.md `description:` frontmatter value is modified in the diff without a corresponding minor (or higher) version bump in the plugin manifest.
severity_rules.description: Changing a SKILL.md description is a contract-visible change consumed by the orchestrator's skill-discovery layer. Without a minor bump, downstream plugin consumers cannot pin against the new behaviour.
severity_rules.remediation: Either revert the description change OR bump the plugin manifest minor version (e.g., 1.4.x -> 1.5.0) in the same PR.

severity_rules.pattern: A SKILL.md file is removed from the diff without a corresponding major version bump in the plugin manifest.
severity_rules.description: Removing a skill is a breaking contract change. Plugin consumers depending on the removed skill will fail at runtime. A major bump is mandatory.
severity_rules.remediation: Either keep the skill (mark deprecated for one cycle) OR bump the plugin manifest major version (e.g., 1.x -> 2.0) in the same PR.

severity_rules.pattern: A command file under `commands/` or an agent file under `agents/` is removed or renamed in the diff without a corresponding major version bump in the plugin manifest.
severity_rules.description: Removing or renaming a command/agent breaks every consumer that invokes it by name. A major bump is mandatory; renames are equivalent to remove + add for compatibility purposes.
severity_rules.remediation: Either keep the original command/agent name as a deprecation alias for one cycle OR bump the plugin manifest major version in the same PR.

severity_rules.pattern: The plugin manifest schema (`manifest.yaml` shape, required fields, or value vocabulary) is changed in the diff without a corresponding major version bump.
severity_rules.description: Manifest schema changes invalidate consumer parsing logic. A major bump is mandatory.
severity_rules.remediation: Either keep the existing manifest shape (additive, optional fields only) OR bump the plugin manifest major version in the same PR.

severity_rules.pattern: A required field is added to a frontmatter contract (e.g., `story-template.md`, `plugin-frontmatter-validator` ruleset) in the diff without a corresponding major version bump.
severity_rules.description: Adding a required frontmatter field breaks every existing artifact that does not yet carry it. A major bump is mandatory; additive optional fields are not flagged.
severity_rules.remediation: Either make the new field optional (with a sensible default) OR bump the plugin manifest major version in the same PR.

severity_rules.pattern: The diff contains only bug fixes (no contract changes — no SKILL.md description edits, no removed skills/commands/agents, no manifest schema changes, no frontmatter required-field additions). The rubric infers a patch bump is sufficient.
severity_rules.description: A bug-fix-only PR does not require a major or minor bump. The patch level is sufficient. This rule is advisory — emitted as Info to document the inferred bump level for transparency.
severity_rules.remediation: Apply a patch bump (e.g., 1.4.1 -> 1.4.2) in the plugin manifest. No higher bump is required.

severity_rules.pattern: An `adapter.json` file declares a `category` value that is not in the 14-value canonical enum defined at `adapters/_schema/adapter.schema.json` (`linter`, `formatter`, `type-checker`, `sast`, `secret-scan`, `dep-audit`, `dast`, `e2e-runner`, `perf-tool`, `a11y-scanner`, `mobile-static`, `mobile-dynamic`, `device-farm`, `deploy`).
severity_rules.description: An unvetted category value sneaking into the registry undermines tool-selection precedence and represents a supply-chain risk surface. The hygiene check fires as a WARNING; the adapter author either picks an in-enum value or proposes an enum extension via a separate PR.
severity_rules.remediation: Pick a category from the 14-value canonical enum, OR open a follow-up PR that extends `adapter.schema.json#/properties/category/enum` with rationale (and update this rubric's `metadata.adapter_category_canonical_enum.values` to match).
